channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Preview ESO's Mysterious Night Market Event Zone, Factions, Districts, and Even a Brand New House to Earn
item.content:encoded:
The mysterious Night Market event zone is coming later this month to The Elder Scrolls Online, and the ZOS team has a preview of what to expect, how this first-ever “event zone” works, what you’ll face, and what you can earn.

Since this is an event zone, when the Night Market appears, it will be for a limited time, close, and then return sometime later, with changes. The mystery is part of the lore and it looks to be part of how those changes, including maybe a change of location, may happen in the future.

This first one will be available starting April 29th and close on June 17th. It will open up in the Oblivion realm of Fargrave. There will be three distinct districts within, with three factions to contend with, each with their own motives and goals. 

This time, you’ll meet the Ruckus, the Thousand Eyes, and the Glittering Goad. Not only does each faction have their own motivations, they all want access to the wealth that the market may offer. You have a decision to make, and that is which one to align with, earn Favor, and unlock associated benefits and rewards for doing so. Once you lock in a decision, it's permanent until the next market. 

There are three districts: Sorrow’s Friend, the Skittering Precinct, and the Parch. A neutral hub, Starlit Plaza, is where you can take on the introductory quest (and even earn a new house just for completing it). 

The ESO team warns more than once that this is designed to be very tough content, so you'll want to team up with others. Fight tough bosses and all-new threats, sure, but also be prepared to solve puzzles, handle difficult terrain (like the “perpetual sandstorms” of the Parch), and collect Relic Splinters, fragments of district relics that, when unlocked, open up previously-inaccessible pathways. Do all of this and continue to earn Favor for your faction. 

Oh and that house you earned, the Night’s Den House, has  a treasure room and the dynamic setup where the visuals change as you increase your wealth and as you unlock district relics.

While the ESO team  isn't giving away all of the mysteries, you can check out the preview for a lot more on this upcoming event zone. If you want a taste in advance, The Night Market and some content from the rest of Season Zero and Season One is now on the PTS. 

Christina Gonzalez

Christina is MMORPG.COM’s News Editor and a contributor since 2011. Always a fan of great community and wondering if the same sort of magic that was her first guild exists anymore.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/preview-esos-mysterious-night-market-event-zone-factions-districts-and-even-a-brand-new-house-to-earn-2000137819
